beats.full_text: If a hot face is familiar, this shame pattern needs a better reset.
beats.ssot_text: If a hot face is familiar, this shame pattern needs a better reset.

beats.full_text: Trying to outrun shame usually makes the loop more expensive. More hiding, more over-explaining. And less chance anyone can actually help.
beats.ssot_text: Trying to outrun shame usually makes the loop more expensive: more hiding, more over-explaining, and less chance anyone can actually help.

beats.speakable_text: Trying to outrun shame usually makes the loop more expensive. More hiding, more over-explaining. And less chance anyone can actually help.

beats.full_text: Shame often persists because the nervous system treats being seen clearly as a threat to belonging. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.
beats.ssot_text: Shame often persists because the nervous system treats being seen clearly as a threat to belonging. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.

beats.speakable_text: Shame often persists because the nervous system treats being seen clearly as a threat to belonging. Pushing harder can look like progress while the underlying pattern stays the same.

beats.full_text: Try this. Name the actual event out loud in one sentence, set it apart from your worth in a second sentence. And choose one honest next step. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.
beats.ssot_text: Try this: name the actual event out loud in one sentence, separate it from your worth in a second sentence, and choose one honest next step. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.

beats.speakable_text: Try this. Name the actual event out loud in one sentence, set it apart from your worth in a second sentence. And choose one honest next step. Keep it small enough to finish before your brain starts negotiating.

beats.full_text: Bookmark the pattern. Signal, reset, next action. The mistake is information about a moment, not a verdict on who you are.
